summary: Russian forces reported liberating Dobropasovo in Dnipropetrovsk region and intensified strikes on Kyiv facilities. Ukrainian forces continue defending front‑line villages, notably Mala Tokmachka, marking a record‑long hold. Civilian casualties from recent Russian attacks in Kyiv and Kharkiv regions remain high.
key_points: Russian Ministry of Defense claims liberation of Dobropasovo near Pokrovskoe, Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30415).
Ukrainian forces in Mala Tokmachka, Zaporizhzhia region, have held the line for over 1500 days, recognized by the 118th Separate Mechanized Brigade (Source: @UkraineNow/81449).
Russian air strikes on a residential building in Kyiv's Shevchenkivskyi district killed 2 civilians, injured 92 (including 3 children), and damaged a five‑storey block (Source: @UkraineNow/81458).
A Russian missile attack on Derhachi (Kharkiv region) wounded 19 people, killed a 68‑year‑old man, and damaged a civilian enterprise (Source: @UkraineNow/81448).
Ukrainian officials report an 18‑year‑old detained near a Ministry of Defence site for allegedly mapping Russian strike locations during the May 24 mass attack on Kyiv (Source: @UkraineNow/81454).

Multiple UAV sightings reported across northern and eastern oblasts, including a group heading south from Chernihiv past Slavutych and UAVs moving northwest over Dnipropetrovsk (Source: @kpszsu/62707, @kpszsu/62760).
Russian Defense Ministry announces sequential strikes on Ukrainian defense industry enterprises in Kyiv (Source: @tass_agency/377493).

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces claim to have liberated Dobropasovo in Dnipropetrovsk region and are conducting sequential strikes on Kyiv defense‑industry targets. Ukrainian defenders maintain a record‑long hold on Mala Tokmachka despite ongoing artillery and UAV activity across multiple fronts. Civilian casualties continue to rise from Russian missile and bomb attacks in Kyiv and Kharkiv regions.

points: Ukrainian troops of the 118th Separate Mechanized Brigade have defended the village of Mala Tokmachka in Zaporizhzhia region for more than 1500 days, marking one of the longest‑held front‑line positions (Source: @UkraineNow/81449).

In Kyiv's Shevchenkivskyi district, a Russian strike on a residential building resulted in 2 civilian deaths, 92 injuries (including three children), and the destruction of a five‑storey block; 13 people were rescued and 320 sought psychological aid (Source: @UkraineNow/81458).

An 18‑year‑old was detained near a Ministry of Defence facility in Kyiv after authorities found communications indicating he was mapping Russian strike movements during the May 24 mass attack (Source: @UkraineNow/81454).

Ukrainian authorities report 19 casualties in Derhachi, Kharkiv region, after a Russian missile hit a civilian enterprise, killing a 68‑year‑old man and wounding 18 others (Source: @UkraineNow/81448).
The Russian Ministry of Defense announced the liberation of the village of Dobropasovo near Pokrovskoe in Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30415).
Russian Aerospace Forces are conducting sequential strikes on Ukrainian defense‑industry facilities in Kyiv, as stated by the Russian Foreign Ministry (Source: @tass_agency/377493).
Russian officials reported that Bulgaria supplied large amounts of weapons to Ukraine, describing it as a major mistake (Source: @tass_agency/377523).
Russian Foreign Ministry indicated plans to file a complaint at the International Court of Justice regarding alleged persecution of ethnic Russians in the Baltic states (Source: @UkraineNow/81453).
UAV groups were observed moving southeast toward Izium and Balakliia in eastern Kharkiv region (Source: @kpszsu/62738).
Guided aviation bombs were launched by hostile tactical aviation targeting Sumy region (Source: @kpszsu/62736).
A UAV group operated in the western part of Kharkiv oblast, passing near Kovyiaky and Sharivka, heading southwest (Source: @kpszsu/62755).
Multiple UAV sightings reported in Sumy oblast, including locations Yampil, Trostyanets, and Lebedyn (Source: @eRadarrua/93685).
UAVs were sighted in Chernihiv region passing near Slavutych, heading south (Source: @kpszsu/62707).
A UAV group moved northward from Kharkiv region, passing Kotelva, heading southwest (Source: @kpszsu/62711).
UAV activity reported in Poltava region moving toward Dykanka and Opishnya (Source: @eRadarrua/93629).
UAVs detected in Poltava region heading toward Zaturyne, Sosnivka, Vorizhba, and Krolevech in Sumy, and toward Krasnokutsk in Kharkiv (Source: @eRadarrua/93661).
UAVs observed over Kherson region moving northwest (Source: @kpszsu/62766).
UAVs reported in southern‑west Sumy region heading toward Romny (Source: @kpszsu/62746).
UAV activity noted in Chernihiv region over Ripky (Source: @eRadarrua/93685).
UAVs detected in the Black Sea near Odesa region, described as hostile reconnaissance UAVs (Source: @kpszsu/62737).
Two Tu‑95MS strategic bombers were reported relocating from Olenya airfield to the Far East (Source: @eRadarrua/93655).
Temporary flight restrictions were imposed at Kaliningrad airport (Source: @tass_agency/377515).
Russian officials announced systematic strikes on Kyiv facilities used by Ukrainian forces (Source: @tass_agency/377493).
Ukrainian officials cited upcoming additional assistance to strengthen air defence systems from the European Commission (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30412).

key_developments: Russian Ministry of Defense claims the village of Dobropasovo near Pokrovskoe in Dnipropetrovsk region has been liberated (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30415).
Ukrainian 118th Separate Mechanized Brigade records a record‑long defense of Mala Tokmachka, holding the line for over 1500 days (Source: @UkraineNow/81449).
A Russian missile strike on a residential building in Kyiv's Shevchenkivskyi district killed 2 civilians and injured 92, including three children (Source: @UkraineNow/81458).
Russian forces conducted a missile attack on Derhachi, Kharkiv region, causing 19 casualties and killing a 68‑year‑old man (Source: @UkraineNow/81448).
Ukrainian authorities detained an 18‑year‑old near a Ministry of Defence site for allegedly mapping Russian strike locations during the May 24 mass attack on Kyiv (Source: @UkraineNow/81454).

Multiple UAV groups were observed across northern and eastern fronts, including movements toward Izium, Balakliia, and Sumy, indicating sustained aerial reconnaissance activity (Source: @kpszsu/62738, @kpszsu/62755).
Russian Ministry of Defence announced sequential strikes on Ukrainian defense‑industry enterprises in Kyiv (Source: @tass_agency/377493).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian operational tempo remains high, with claims of territorial gains in the east and intensified air strikes on Kyiv's infrastructure. UAV proliferation across multiple fronts suggests enhanced Russian reconnaissance and targeting capabilities, complicating Ukrainian defensive operations. Civilian casualty rates are rising due to repeated missile and bomb attacks on populated areas, indicating a continued threat to non‑military targets.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is reinforcing front‑line units, highlighted by the record defense at Mala Tokmachka, while also tightening security around key ministries in Kyiv following the detention of a civilian collaborator. International partners, including the EU, have pledged further air‑defence aid, though specific delivery timelines remain unspecified. Russian diplomatic channels are pursuing legal actions against Baltic states, reflecting a broader strategy to pressure Ukraine's allies.
sections.intelligence_notes: UAV flight paths provide actionable intelligence on Russian targeting priorities, especially around logistics hubs in Sumy and Kharkiv. The reported liberation of Dobropasovo may indicate a localized offensive but requires verification. Increased civilian targeting in Kyiv suggests a possible shift toward psychological and infrastructural pressure. Monitoring of Russian legal and diplomatic maneuvers could reveal future escalation vectors.
